text: β-alanine supplementation has a small but significant ergogenic effect on exercise capacity and performance overall.
β-alanine supplementation is most effective for exercise lasting 0.5 to 10 minutes, with exercise duration significantly moderating the ergogenic effect.
β-alanine has a greater effect on exercise capacity (time to exhaustion) than on exercise performance (time trials or competition) within the 0.5-10 minute exercise window.
Co-supplementation of β-alanine with sodium bicarbonate produces a larger ergogenic effect than β-alanine alone.
Training status does not significantly moderate the ergogenic effect of β-alanine supplementation, meaning both trained and untrained individuals may benefit similarly.
The total amount of β-alanine ingested does not significantly moderate the ergogenic effect, suggesting a threshold rather than dose-response relationship.
β-alanine supplementation requires chronic ingestion to be effective, as skeletal muscle carnosine has a long washout period after supplementation ceases.
